seoTitle: One Tree vs Ooma — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between One Tree and Ooma. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: One Tree vs Ooma: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in One Tree and Ooma.
heading: One Tree vs Ooma — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between One Tree, New South Wales and Ooma, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ooma has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to One Tree (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is One Tree or Ooma safer from flooding?
faq.answer: One Tree has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Ooma scores 24/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: One Tree scores 56/100 for bushfire risk. Ooma scores 30/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in One Tree or Ooma?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ooma has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
